1: Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Yah has treasured, four Isr. Or Tsphanyahuw; from tsaphan and Yahh; Jah has secreted; Tsephanjah, the name of four Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Desc. of Cush. Patronymically from Kuwsh; a Cushite, or descendant of Cush.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Or Gdalyahuw; from gadal and Yahh; Jah has become great; Gedaljah, the name of five Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Or prolonged tamaryahuw; from 'amar and Yahh; Jah has said; Amarjah, the name of nine Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Yah has strengthened, a king of Judah, also several other Isr.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Yah supports, two Isr. Or; from the same root as 'ashuwyah and Yahh; founded of Jah; Joshijah, the name of two Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Masterworkman, three Isr. The same as 'amown; Amon, the name of three Israelites.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
2: To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
To come to an end, cease. A primitive root; to snatch away, i.e. Terminate.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
3: To come to an end, cease. A primitive root; to snatch away, i.e. Terminate.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
To come to an end, cease. A primitive root; to snatch away, i.e. Terminate.
Flying creatures. From uwph; a bird, often collectively.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
A fish. Or dag; from dagah; a fish; or perhaps rather from da'ag; but still better from da'ag; a fish.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
Something overthrown, a stumbling block. Feminine from kashal; a stumbling-block, but only figuratively (idol).
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
4: A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
Rest, residue, remnant. From sha'ar; a remainder.
Baal -- a heathen god. The same as ba'al; Baal, a Phoenician deity.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
A priest (in idol worship). From kamar; properly, an ascetic, i.e. An idolatrous priest.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Priest. Active participle of kahan; literally, one officiating, a priest; also an acting priest.
5: Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A roof, a top. Probably by reduplication from ga'ah; a roof; by analogy, the top of an altar.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
A primitive root; propr. To be complete, but used only as a denominative from sheba'; to seven oneself, i.e. Swear.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A primitive root; propr. To be complete, but used only as a denominative from sheba'; to seven oneself, i.e. Swear.
6: Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To move away, backslide. A primitive root; properly, to flinch, i.e. to go back, literally or figuratively.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow; by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship.
7: Hush! keep silence! A primitive root; to hush.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Near. Or qarob; from qarab; near.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
To be set apart or consecrated. A primitive root; to be clean.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
To attend to, visit, muster, appoint. A primitive root; to visit; by analogy, to oversee, muster, charge, care for, miss, deposit, etc.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
Foreign, alien. From neker; strange, in a variety of degrees and applications.
Raiment, attire. Or malbush; from labash; a garment, or clothing.

Threshold. From the same as pethen; a stretcher, i.e. A sill.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Lord. Or adon; from an unused root; sovereign, i.e. Controller.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Deceit, treachery. From ramah in the sense of deceiving; fraud.
10: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A cry, outcry. From tsa'aq; a shriek.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A fish. Or dag; from dagah; a fish; or perhaps rather from da'ag; but still better from da'ag; a fish.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
A howling. Feminine of ylel; a howling.
A double, copy, second. From shanah; properly, a repetition, i.e. A duplicate, or a double; by implication, a second.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
Or sheber; from shabar; a fracture, figuratively, ruin; specifically, a solution.
A hill. Feminine from the same as Geba'; a hillock.
11: To howl, make a howling. A primitive root; to howl or yell.

A place probably in Jer. The same as maktesh; dell; the Maktesh, a place in Jerusalem.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
From kana'; humiliated; Kenaan, a son a Ham; also the country inhabited by him.

Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.

Lees, dregs. From shamar; something preserved, i.e. The settlings of wine.

Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.

To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Devastation, waste. Or shimamah; feminine of shamem; devastation; figuratively, astonishment.
To build. A primitive root; to build.

To plant. A primitive root; properly, to strike in, i.e. Fix; specifically, to plant.

To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.

Near. Or qarob; from qarab; near.

Hastening, speedy, swift. From mahar; properly, hurrying; hence in a hurry.
Muchness, force, abundance. From the same as 'uwd; properly, vehemence, i.e. vehemently; by implication, wholly, speedily, etc.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To cry, roar. A primitive root; to be clear, i.e. To whoop.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
Bitter, bitterness. Or marah; from marar; bitter; also bitterness, or bitterly.

Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

Desolation. Or mshoah; from the same as show'; ruin, abstractly or concretely.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
Darkness, gloominess, calamity. Feminine of 'aphel; duskiness, figuratively, misfortune; concrete, concealment.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A cloud mass, cloud. From anan; a cloud, i.e. The nimbus or thunder-cloud.
Cloud, heavy cloud. Probably from araph; gloom.

A horn (for blowing). Or shophar; from shaphar in the original sense of incising; a cornet or curved horn.

High, exalted. Or gabowahh; from gabahh; elevated, powerful, arrogant.

17: A primitive root; to cramp, literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.

Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
To pour out, pour. A primitive root; to spill forth; also to expend; intensively, to sprawl out.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
Intestines, bowels. Or lachum; passive participle of lacham; properly, eaten, i.e. Food; also flesh, i.e. Body.
18: Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Ardor, zeal, jealousy. From qana'; jealousy or envy.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
Completion, complete destruction, consumption, annihilation. From kalah; a completion; adverb, completely; also destruction.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.

Poor, afflicted, humble, meek. Or Aanayv; from anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.

4: A Philistine city. Feminine of az; strong; Azzah, a place in Palestine.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
A city of the Philistines. Probably from shaqal in the sense of weighing-place; Ashkelon, a place in Palestine.
Devastation, waste. Or shimamah; feminine of shamem; devastation; figuratively, astonishment.

Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From kana'; humiliated; Kenaan, a son a Ham; also the country inhabited by him.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Inhab. of Philistia. Patrial from Plesheth; a Pelishtite or inhabitant of Pelesheth.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
6: A rope, a measuring line, a district, inheritance, a noose, a company, a throe, ruin.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.
A cistern, well. Feminine of kar; a meadow.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
A wall. Feminine of gader; enclosure.
Small cattle, sheep and goats, flock. Or tsaown; from an unused root meaning to migrate; a collective name for a flock; also figuratively.
7: A rope, a measuring line, a district, inheritance, a noose, a company, a throe, ruin.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Rest, residue, remnant, remainder. From sha'ar; a remainder or residual portion.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
A primitive root; to crouch; be implication, to recline, repose, brood, lurk, imbed.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
A city of the Philistines. Probably from shaqal in the sense of weighing-place; Ashkelon, a place in Palestine.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To attend to, visit, muster, appoint. A primitive root; to visit; by analogy, to oversee, muster, charge, care for, miss, deposit, etc.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Captivity, captives. Or shbiyth; from shabah; exile, concretely, prisoners; figuratively, a former state of prosperity.
8: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
A son of Lot, also his desc. and the territory where they settled.
Revilings, reviling words. Or gidduph; and gidduphah; or gidduphah; from gduwphah; vilification.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
A people living E. of the Jordan. From am; tribal, i.e. Inbred; Ammon, a son of Lot; also his posterity and their country.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To pull off, to expose, to betroth, to carp at, defame, to spend the winter.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Border, boundary, territory. Or gbul; from gabal; properly, a cord, i.e. a boundary; by extens. The territory inclosed.
9: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
A son of Lot, also his desc. and the territory where they settled.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A Canaanite city near the Dead Sea. From an unused root meaning to scorch; burnt district; Sedom, a place near the Dead Sea.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
A people living E. of the Jordan. From am; tribal, i.e. Inbred; Ammon, a son of Lot; also his posterity and their country.
A city in the Jordan Valley. From amar; a heap; Amorah, a place in Palestine.
Perhaps possession. From the same as mesheq; a possession.
A pit. From karah; a pit.
Salt. From malach; properly, powder, i.e. salt
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Devastation, waste. Or shimamah; feminine of shamem; devastation; figuratively, astonishment.
Rest, residue, remnant, remainder. From sha'ar; a remainder or residual portion.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To spoil, plunder. A primitive root; to plunder.
From yathar; properly, an overhanging, i.e. an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
A primitive root; to inherit (figurative) mode of descent), or to occupy; causatively, to bequeath, or distribute, instate.
10: Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Arrogance, majesty, ornament. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.
To pull off, to expose, to betroth, to carp at, defame, to spend the winter.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To be or grow lean. A primitive root; to emaciate, i.e. Make thin.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Coast, region. From 'avah; properly, a habitable spot; dry land, a coast, an island.
To bow down. A primitive root; to depress, i.e. Prostrate.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
12: Desc. of Cush. Patronymically from Kuwsh; a Cushite, or descendant of Cush.
Pierced, polluted. From chalal; pierced; figuratively, polluted.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
13: A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
North. Or tsaphon; from tsaphan; properly, hidden, i.e. Dark; used only of the north as a quarter.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them, its region and its empire.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Capital of Assyr. Of foreign origin; Nineveh, the capital of Assyria.
Devastation, waste. Or shimamah; feminine of shamem; devastation; figuratively, astonishment.
Dryness, drought. From an unused root meaning to parch; aridity; concretely, a desert.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
14: A flock, herd. From adar; an arrangement, i.e. Muster.
A primitive root; to crouch; be implication, to recline, repose, brood, lurk, imbed.
Midst. From an unused root meaning to sever; a bisection, i.e. the centre.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
(a bird) perhaps pelican. From qow'; probably the pelican.
Porcupine. Or qippod; from qaphad; a species of bird, perhaps the bittern.
Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
To sing. Or shuwr; a primitive root; to sing.
Dryness, drought, heat. A collaterally form of chereb; drought or desolation.
A vestibule, a dish. From caphaph, in its original sense of containing; a vestibule; also a dish.
To be naked or bare. A primitive root; to be bare; hence, to empty, pour out, demolish.
Cedar panels, cedar work. Fem of 'erez; cedar wainscoating.
15: Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Security. From batach; properly, a place of refuge; abstract, safety, both the fact and the feeling; often safely.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
From 'aphec; cessation, i.e. An end; often used adverb, no further; also the ankle, as being the extremity of the leg or foot.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
Waste, horror. From shamem; ruin; by implication, consternation.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
(place of) lying down. From rabats; a reclining place, i.e. Fold.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To hiss, whistle, pipe. A primitive root; properly, to be shrill, i.e. To whistle or hiss.
A primitive root; to waver, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively.

1: Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
To oppress, maltreat, perhaps suppress. A primitive root; to rage or be violent: by implication, to suppress, to maltreat.
Perhaps to flap (the wings). A primitive root; to rebel; hence to whip, i.e. Lash.
To defile. A primitive root,; to soil or desecrate.
2: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To come near, approach. A primitive root; to approach for whatever purpose.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
3: Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
To roar. A primitive root; to rumble or moan.
A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
A wolf. From an unused root meaning to be yellow; a wolf.
A primitive root; to be spare or skeleton-like; used only as a denominative from gerem; to bone, i.e. Denude the bones.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
4: A spokesman, speaker, prophet. From naba'; a prophet or inspired man.
To be wanton or reckless. A primitive root; to bubble up or froth, i.e. to be unimportant.
Treachery. Feminine plural active participle of bagad; treacheries.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
Priest. Active participle of kahan; literally, one officiating, a priest; also an acting priest.
A primitive root; properly, to bore, i.e. to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane, to break, to begin; denom. to play.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
To treat violently or wrong. A primitive root; to be violent; by implication, to maltreat.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
5: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to arrange, as a battle, a vineyard; hence, to muster and so to miss.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Unjust, unrighteous one. Intensive from aval; evil.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Shame, shameful thing. From buwsh; shame; by implication an idol.
6: To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
A corner. Feminine of pen; an angle; by implication, a pinnacle; figuratively, a chieftain.
To be desolated or appalled. A primitive root; to stun, i.e. Devastate or stupefy.
To parch, to desolate, destroy, kill. Or chareb; a primitive root; to parch i.e. to desolate, destroy, kill.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
To chase, to desolate. A primitive root; to chase; by implication, to desolate.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
7: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
Dwelling, habitation. Or maiyn; from the same as ownah; an abode, of God, men or animals; hence, a retreat.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To attend to, visit, muster, appoint. A primitive root; to visit; by analogy, to oversee, muster, charge, care for, miss, deposit, etc.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Surely. From kuwn; firmly; figuratively, surely; also But.
A primitive root; properly, to incline; but used only as denominative from shkem; literally, to load up, i.e. To start early in the morning.
Perhaps to go to ruin. A primitive root; to decay, i.e. ruin.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Or malilah; from alal in the sense of effecting; an exploit, or a performance; by implication, an opportunity.
8: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To wait, await. A primitive root; properly, to adhere to; hence, to await.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Booty, prey. The same as ad in the sense of the aim of an attack; booty.
To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To gather, collect. A primitive root; to grasp, i.e. Collect.
Kingdom, sovereignty, dominion, reign. From malak; dominion, i.e. the estate or the country.
To pour out, pour. A primitive root; to spill forth; also to expend; intensively, to sprawl out.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Indignation. From za'am; strictly froth at the mouth, i.e. fury.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
(burning of) anger. Or charon; from charah; a burning of anger.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Ardor, zeal, jealousy. From qana'; jealousy or envy.
9: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
To purify, select. A primitive root; to clarify, examine, select.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To work, serve. A primitive root; to work; by implication, to serve, till, enslave, etc.
Shoulder. From shakam; the neck as the place of burdens; figuratively, the spur of a hill.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
10: Region across or beyond, side. From abar; properly, a region across; but used only adverbially on the opposite side.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
Probably of foreign origin; Cush, the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite.
Incense, a worshipper. From athar; incense; hence a worshipper.
To be dispersed or scattered. A primitive root; to dash in pieces, literally or figuratively.
To conduct, bear along. A primitive root; properly, to flow; causatively, to bring.
From an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. Bestow; a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering.
11: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Or malilah; from alal in the sense of effecting; an exploit, or a performance; by implication, an opportunity.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To rebel, transgress. A primitive root; to break away, i.e. Trespass, apostatize, quarrel.
At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Majesty, pride. From ga'ah; arrogance or majesty; by implication, ornament.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To soar, be lofty, to be haughty. A primitive root; to soar, i.e. Be lofty; figuratively, to be haughty.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
12: To remain, be left over. A primitive root; properly, to swell up, i.e. Be redundant.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To seek refuge. A primitive root; to flee for protection; figuratively, to confide in.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
13: Rest, residue, remnant, remainder. From sha'ar; a remainder or residual portion.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
A lie, falsehood, deceptive thing. From kazab; falsehood; literally or figuratively.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Perhaps treachery. And tarmuwth; or tarmiyth; from ramah; fraud.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
A primitive root; to crouch; be implication, to recline, repose, brood, lurk, imbed.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To tremble, be terrified. A primitive root; to shudder with terror; hence, to fear; also to hasten.
14: To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
To raise a shout, give a blast. A primitive root; to mar; figuratively, to split the ears, i.e. Shout.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
To exult. A primitive root; to jump for joy, i.e. Exult.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
15: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
To turn. A primitive root; to turn; by implication, to face, i.e. Appear, look, etc.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
16: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Sink, relax. A primitive root; to slacken.
17: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
To exult, rejoice. Or siys; a primitive root; to be bright, i.e. Cheerful.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
Love (noun). Feminine of 'ahab and meaning the same.
To rejoice. Or guwl; a primitive root; properly, to spin round, i.e. Usually rejoice, or fear.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A ringing cry. From ranan; properly, a creaking, i.e. Shout.
18: To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
To suffer. A primitive root; to grieve.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
19: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To be bowed down or afflicted. A primitive root; to depress literally or figuratively, transitive or intransitive.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To limp. A primitive root: probably to curve; used only as denominative from tsela', to limp.
To gather, collect. A primitive root; to grasp, i.e. Collect.
To impel, thrust, banish. A primitive root; to push off; used in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Praise, song of praise. From halal; laudation; specifically a hymn.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Shame, shameful thing. From buwsh; shame; by implication an idol.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
20: Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To gather, collect. A primitive root; to grasp, i.e. Collect.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Praise, song of praise. From halal; laudation; specifically a hymn.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Captivity, captives. Or shbiyth; from shabah; exile, concretely, prisoners; figuratively, a former state of prosperity.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
